evals.name: scaffold-verify-node
evals.grounding: evalsPlan §B Phase-1 gate: eval-node fixture (pnpm+vitest, L1 present, L0+L2 absent). 5 deterministic checks.
evals.prompt: 진단해서 두 축(계층 wall-clock·flake·purpose-fidelity)으로 리포트하고, 빠진 계층 스캐폴드하고, verify 엔트리 노출해줘.

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (1): verify.sh exists, chmod +x, --help exits 0 and prints detected stack
checks.desc: scripts/verify.sh is executable and --help prints the detected JS/Node stack

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (2): scripts/verify.sh exits 0 (green) on the fixture
checks.desc: scripts/verify.sh runs all present layers and exits 0 (green) on eval-node

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (3): --json emits valid NDJSON with one line per present layer + aggregate carrying changedFeedbackMs/fullSuiteMs/flakeRatePct/purposeGapCount
checks.cmd: scripts/verify.sh --json | node -e "const lines=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n'); const parsed=lines.map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const agg=parsed.find(r=>r.summary===true); if(!agg) throw new Error('no aggregate line'); ['changedFeedbackMs','fullSuiteMs','flakeRatePct','purposeGapCount'].forEach(k=>{ if(!(k in agg)) throw new Error('missing '+k); }); const layers=parsed.filter(r=>!r.summary); if(layers.length===0) throw new Error('no per-layer lines');"
checks.desc: --json emits valid NDJSON: per-layer lines + aggregate with the 4 budget numbers (changedFeedbackMs, fullSuiteMs, flakeRatePct, purposeGapCount)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (4): --scope <token> exits 0 for a valid token from --list-scopes; bogus token exits 2 (zero-match contract gap, NOT 1). Token must match /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ (recursive-slice.js:611).
checks.cmd: SCOPE=$(scripts/verify.sh --list-scopes | head -1) && test -n "$SCOPE" && scripts/verify.sh --scope "$SCOPE" --layer l1 && BOGUS_EXIT=0; scripts/verify.sh --scope bogus_token_that_matches_nothing_xyz 2>/dev/null; BOGUS_EXIT=$?; test "$BOGUS_EXIT" -eq 2

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (5): diagnose report JSON validates against diagnoseReportSchema; absent layers marked present:false (no false green). eval-node has L0 present, L1 present, L2 absent, L3 absent.
checks.cmd: scripts/verify.sh --json | node -e "const lines=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n').map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const l2=lines.find(r=>r.layer==='l2'); const l3=lines.find(r=>r.layer==='l3'); if(!l2||l2.present!==false) throw new Error('L2 must be present:false'); if(!l3||l3.present!==false) throw new Error('L3 must be present:false'); if(l2.passed!==null) throw new Error('absent layer passed must be null'); const agg=lines.find(r=>r.summary===true); if(agg.purposeGapCount<2) throw new Error('purposeGapCount must count absent L2+L3');"
checks.desc: Absent L2 and L3 are present:false with purposeGap set; purposeGapCount>=2 in aggregate (no false green)

evals.name: scaffold-verify-python
evals.grounding: evalsPlan §B Phase-1 gate: eval-python fixture (uv+pytest, L1 present, L0 absent). 5 deterministic checks.
evals.prompt: 진단해서 두 축(계층 wall-clock·flake·purpose-fidelity)으로 리포트하고, 빠진 계층 스캐폴드하고, verify 엔트리 노출해줘.

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (1): verify.sh exists, chmod +x, --help exits 0 and prints detected stack
checks.desc: scripts/verify.sh is executable and --help prints the detected Python stack

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (2): scripts/verify.sh exits 0 (green) on the fixture
checks.desc: scripts/verify.sh runs all present layers and exits 0 (green) on eval-python

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (3): --json emits valid NDJSON with one line per present layer + aggregate carrying the 4 budget numbers
checks.cmd: scripts/verify.sh --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; lines=[json.loads(l) for l in sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')]; agg=next(r for r in lines if r.get('summary')); missing=[k for k in ['changedFeedbackMs','fullSuiteMs','flakeRatePct','purposeGapCount'] if k not in agg]; assert not missing, f'missing keys: {missing}'; layers=[r for r in lines if not r.get('summary')]; assert layers, 'no per-layer lines'"
checks.desc: --json emits valid NDJSON: per-layer lines + aggregate with the 4 budget numbers

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (4): --scope <token> exits 0 for a valid token; bogus token exits 2. Token is a bare /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ name (e.g. test_add_returns_sum from the fixture). recursive-slice.js:611 constraint.
checks.cmd: SCOPE=$(scripts/verify.sh --list-scopes | head -1) && test -n "$SCOPE" && scripts/verify.sh --scope "$SCOPE" --layer l1 && scripts/verify.sh --scope bogus_xyz_no_match 2>/dev/null; test $? -eq 2

checks.grounding: evalsPlan §B check (5): absent layers present:false, no false green. eval-python has L1 present, L0 absent (no ruff until scaffolded), L2 absent, L3 absent.
checks.cmd: scripts/verify.sh --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; lines=[json.loads(l) for l in sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')]; l2=next((r for r in lines if r.get('layer')=='l2'),None); l3=next((r for r in lines if r.get('layer')=='l3'),None); assert l2 and l2['present']==False, 'L2 must be present:false'; assert l2['passed'] is None, 'absent layer passed must be null'; assert l3 and l3['present']==False, 'L3 must be present:false'; agg=next(r for r in lines if r.get('summary')); assert agg['purposeGapCount']>=1, 'purposeGapCount must be >=1 for absent L2'"
checks.desc: Absent L2/L3 are present:false; purposeGapCount>=1 in aggregate; no false green for absent layers

evals.name: slice-integration-contract
evals.grounding: evalsPlan §C: filterCommand='scripts/verify.sh --scope {scope}' survives replace({scope},token) for a bare /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ token; measureCommand='scripts/verify.sh'. Guards the §5.1-line-104 dual-contract (recursive-slice.js:612,833,886).
evals.prompt: N/A — this is a contract smoke eval, not a prompt eval. Verified by the checks below.

checks.grounding: recursive-slice.js:612 does filterCommand.replace('{scope}', testScope) verbatim. The resulting string must be a valid shell command for any bare token matching /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/.
checks.cmd: node -e "const fc='scripts/verify.sh --scope {scope}'; const token='add_returns_sum'; const result=fc.replace('{scope}',token); if(result!=='scripts/verify.sh --scope add_returns_sum') throw new Error('replace failed: '+result); const re=/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/; if(!re.test(token)) throw new Error('token not bare'); console.log('filterCommand replace OK:', result);"
checks.desc: filterCommand='scripts/verify.sh --scope {scope}' after replace('{scope}','add_returns_sum') yields 'scripts/verify.sh --scope add_returns_sum' — the verbatim shell command the engine runs

checks.grounding: recursive-slice.js:833,886 runs measureCommand verbatim. measureCommand must equal 'scripts/verify.sh' (not 'scripts/verify.sh --json' alone; --json is optional diagnostic mode).
checks.cmd: node -e "const mc='scripts/verify.sh'; if(mc!=='scripts/verify.sh') throw new Error('wrong measureCommand: '+mc); console.log('measureCommand OK:', mc);"
checks.desc: measureCommand='scripts/verify.sh' is the exact string the slice baseliner BASELINE.measureCommand field must carry

checks.grounding: recursive-slice.js:611 gates injected token with scopeSafe=/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/. Token with slash/space/colon is rejected before reaching filterCommand. Skill must ensure test NAMES are bare-token-shaped (no paths/spaces).
checks.cmd: node -e "const scopeSafe=/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/; const valid=['add_returns_sum','test_clamp_bounds_value','TestCalc.test_add']; const invalid=['src/calc','test name with spaces','foo:bar','../escape']; valid.forEach(t=>{ if(!scopeSafe.test(t)) throw new Error('valid token rejected: '+t); }); invalid.forEach(t=>{ if(scopeSafe.test(t)) throw new Error('invalid token accepted: '+t); }); console.log('scope guard OK');"
checks.desc: scopeSafe=/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ accepts bare identifiers (add_returns_sum) and rejects paths/spaces/colons — the engine constraint test fixtures must honor

evals.name: l2-docker-down-not-green-skip
evals.grounding: verify-contract.md §2 [MUST]: Docker down = L2/L3 exit 3, NOT a green skip. Fixture declares testcontainers (detect picks L2=js-testcontainers); Docker absent => --layer l2 must be present:true, passed:false, exit 3, infra:1, with a purposeGap naming Docker. Asserts the contract does NOT silently green-skip an infra-gated layer.
evals.prompt: N/A — negative-path contract eval. Verified by the checks below.

checks.grounding: L2 with Docker absent exits 3 (infra), never 0 (false green) and never 1 (true RED). Run from the fixture dir so REPO_ROOT resolves to it.
checks.desc: scripts/verify.sh --layer l2 exits 3 (infra) when testcontainers is configured but Docker is down — not a green skip

checks.grounding: The L2 NDJSON line must carry present:true, passed:false, exit:3, infra:1 and a purposeGap mentioning Docker. A present:false / passed:true here would be the false green the contract forbids.
checks.cmd: cd evals/fixtures/eval-l2-docker-down && bash ../../../scripts/verify.sh --layer l2 --json | node -e "const ls=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n').map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const l2=ls.find(r=>r.layer==='l2'); if(!l2) throw new Error('no l2 line'); if(l2.present!==true) throw new Error('L2 must be present:true (testcontainers detected), got '+l2.present); if(l2.passed!==false) throw new Error('L2 passed must be false (Docker down), got '+l2.passed); if(l2.exit!==3) throw new Error('L2 exit must be 3, got '+l2.exit); if(!/docker/i.test(l2.purposeGap||'')) throw new Error('purposeGap must name Docker'); const agg=ls.find(r=>r.summary); if(agg.infra!==1) throw new Error('aggregate infra must be 1');"
checks.desc: L2 NDJSON is present:true + passed:false + exit:3 + infra:1 with a Docker purposeGap (no false green skip)

evals.name: changed-routing-layer-selection
evals.grounding: verify-contract.md §4 Stage-2 (IMPLEMENTED): --changed routes by changed-file TYPE — always l0+l1; +l2 when a changed file matches an integration path (*/db/* etc); a layer impact-selected via the file->layer map reports affected:true. A src-only change must NOT select l2. Fixture is copied into a throwaway git repo so the diff is deterministic (independent of the marketplace tree's own status).
evals.prompt: N/A — routing contract eval. Verified by the checks below.

checks.grounding: Changing only src/calc.js (non-integration) keeps --changed at the fast door l0+l1 — l2 NOT selected.
checks.cmd: T=$(mktemp -d); cp -R evals/fixtures/eval-changed-routing/. "$T"/; V="$PWD/scripts/verify.sh"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email e@e && git config user.name e && git add -A && git commit -qm base && echo 'export const add=(a,b)=>a+b+0;' > src/calc.js && "$V" --changed --json 2>/dev/null ) | node -e "const ls=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n').map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const agg=ls.find(r=>r.summary); if(agg.layers.includes('l2')) throw new Error('src-only change must NOT select l2; got '+JSON.stringify(agg.layers));"; rc=$?; rm -rf "$T"; test $rc -eq 0
checks.desc: --changed with a src-only diff selects only l0+l1 (l2 excluded)

checks.grounding: Changing src/db/pool.js (integration path) selects l2 AND reports l2.affected:true (impact-selected via the file->layer map), proving honest affected.
checks.cmd: T=$(mktemp -d); cp -R evals/fixtures/eval-changed-routing/. "$T"/; V="$PWD/scripts/verify.sh"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email e@e && git config user.name e && git add -A && git commit -qm base && echo 'export const pool={connect:()=>false};' > src/db/pool.js && "$V" --changed --json 2>/dev/null ) | node -e "const ls=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n').map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const agg=ls.find(r=>r.summary); if(!agg.layers.includes('l2')) throw new Error('db/ change must select l2; got '+JSON.stringify(agg.layers)); const l2=ls.find(r=>r.layer==='l2'); if(l2.affected!==true) throw new Error('l2.affected must be true (path-selected), got '+l2.affected);"; rc=$?; rm -rf "$T"; test $rc -eq 0
checks.desc: --changed with a src/db/ (integration) diff selects l2 and reports l2.affected:true (honest impact selection)

evals.name: native-runner-first-delegation
evals.grounding: verify-contract.md §7 native-runner-first: when a committed verify:l0 package.json script exists, verify.sh DELEGATES to `npm run verify:l0` instead of running the inline node L0 floor. Fixture's verify:l0 writes a sentinel file proving delegation actually ran; the NDJSON tool field is reported as npm:verify:l0.
evals.prompt: N/A — delegation contract eval. Verified by the checks below.

checks.grounding: verify.sh must report tool=npm:verify:l0 (delegation) AND the script's side-effect sentinel must exist — inline node-syntax floor would do neither.
checks.cmd: cd evals/fixtures/eval-native-delegate && rm -f .verify-l0-delegated && bash ../../../scripts/verify.sh --layer l0 --json | node -e "const ls=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8').trim().split('\n').map(l=>JSON.parse(l)); const l0=ls.find(r=>r.layer==='l0'); if(l0.tool!=='npm:verify:l0') throw new Error('expected delegation tool npm:verify:l0, got '+l0.tool); if(l0.passed!==true) throw new Error('delegated l0 must pass');" && test -f .verify-l0-delegated; rc=$?; rm -f .verify-l0-delegated; test $rc -eq 0
checks.desc: verify.sh --layer l0 delegates to the committed `verify:l0` npm script (tool=npm:verify:l0) and the script's sentinel file proves it ran (not the inline floor)
